description.text:
### Description

I tried to build from the AUR but there are some package conflicts (various node.js stuff) so I grabbed the AppImage. The very first launch I was met with no application and it nuked my audio.

After fixing my audio I tried again this time running from a term to see if there was any output/issues. This time it launched. It will launch fine from a terminal.

I fiddled around with various options and made a .desktop file for it. Launching from a .desktop file shows it launches, the processes and mounts are there but the window never comes up.

summary: feat: Show more information in the Pomodoro timer (the circular ring-based solution).

description.text:
<!--
  Thank you for opening up this pull request! Please read this comment carefully
  and make sure to fill in as much info as possible below as well.

  First, the obligatory checklist. You must make sure to follow this list as
  much as possible to make merging your PR as easy as possible:

  1. I documented all behaviour within the code as far as I could.
  2. This PR targets the develop branch and *not* the master branch.
  3. I have tested my changes extensively and paid extra attention to potential
     cross-platform issues (e./g. Cmd/Ctrl/Super key bindings)
  4. I ran the `yarn lint` and `yarn test` commands successfully
  5. I have added an entry to the CHANGELOG.md
  6. I matched my code-style to the repository as far as possible
  7. I agree that my code will be published under the GNUP GPL v3 license
  8. In case I created new files, I added a header to them (see the existing
     files for examples)
  9. Before opening this PR, I ran `git pull` a last time to prevent any merge
     issues

  If you don't know how to fulfill some of the above points, feel free to open
  your PR nevertheless and mention those points where you are unsure. The more
  you can fulfill, the faster we can merge your PR, otherwise it will just take
  longer.
 -->

## Description
Closes #5668 
This PR adds the proposed circular solution using two circular rings to display more information in the Pomodoro timer. The outer circle shows the current phase's progression, while the inner circle shows all the phases and an indicator through the entire cycle. The counter at the centre keeps a tally of the work sessions completed so far. 

## Changes
In the PopoverPomodoro.vue file, I modified the PopoverPomodoro.vue file to include and customize and create the SVG circular rings and visualize the progress. The phases in the inner circle remain muted until completed, and tooltips are enabled so that the user can get more information upon hovering. I've also added a text at the centre showing the total number of completed work sessions.

In the App.vue file, I added a new 'totalTasks' field to the 'pomodoro' object to track cumulative work sessions. I've also modified the 'pomodoroTick()' to increment 'totalTasks' and reset 'counter' after a full 8-phase Pomodoro cycle.

## Additional information
<!-- If there is anything else that might be of interest, please provide it here -->

<!-- Please provide any testing system -->
Tested on: Ubuntu 24.04.1 LTS (noble) via WSL2 on Windows 11
Tested and working successfully.

summary: Positive Lookbehind/Lookahead as Pattern for Zettelkasten IDs produces unexpected results

description.text:
### Description

While negative lookbehind/lookahead seem to work as expected in a pattern for Zettelkasten IDs, positive lookbehind/lookahead produce very unexpected results. It seems to me that for the purpose of creating an [[internal link]], Zettlr recognizes that the page linked to has the appropriate ID (as matched by the negative lookbehind/lookahead). For example, if my pattern for Zettelkasten IDs is `((?<=ID: ).*)`, and file2.md has the line `ID: this_is_file_2` as its first line, then linking to file2.md from file1.md will create the link `[[this_is_file_2]]`. For the purpose of _following_ a link though, Zettlr _does not_ recognize that the page linked to has the appropriate ID (as matched by the negative lookbehind/lookahead). In the example above, hovering `[[this_is_file_2]]` brings up the message `File this_is_file_2 does not exist.`

I expected that either:
- Negative lookbehind/lookahead were not supported, in which case attempting to link to file2.md would not use the ID and instead would use the file's name, OR
- Negative lookbehind/lookahead were supported, in which case attempting to link to file2.md would use the ID and then following that link would be possible.

I did not expect in either case that attempting to link to file2.md would create a link that cannot be followed.

### Reproducing

1. Open Zettlr
2. In Preferences, set the Pattern for Zettelkasten IDs to `((?<=ID: ).*)`.
3. In Zettlr, create a file1.md.
4. In Zettlr, create a file2.md.
5. In file2.md, insert the text `ID: this_is_file_2`. 
6. Save file2.md.
7. In file1.md, type `[[file2` and hit the tab button to complete the link. The link reads `[[this_is_file_2]]`.
8. Attempt to follow the link, either by hovering or by control clicking. The link is not followed successfully.

### Zettlr version

3.4.4

### Installation Method

Flathub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [x] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Architecture

- [ ] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Windows 10, Fedora 41, Fedora 42

### Additional Information

I looked through the code trying to figure out what the issue was, but as I'm not a programmer and only have a passing familiarity with code, I wasn't able to identify where the regex was going wrong.

summary: Question about notes thumbnail/card view dashboard if its possible along with timestamping lectures.

description.text:
### Checklist

- [x] Zettlr does not possess this feature (if it does, but the feature doesn't work out for you, propose an enhancement instead.)
- [x] Zettlr's current features cannot be used to approximate this behaviour (otherwise, propose an enhancement instead.)
- [x] I have searched the documentation to make sure I did not overlook it.
- [x] I have searched both open and closed issues to see if someone has already proposed this.

### Feature Proposal

Would it be possible to embed notes with videos like lectures in mp4 or lecture slides from a local source using something like file:/// so it doesn't duplicate and those lectures and slides show up as thumbnails/cards in like a dashboard so I can quickly find things visually? I've been trying to do this in zettlr because I've moved from obsidian to zettlr because I love it and I like the blog posts of the maintainer while I do not trust obsidian because they are not open source. In Obsidian I made a thing where I can setup a timestamp to the lecture video so I can quickly timestamp it and write notes about it and clicking it goes to the timestamp in the video and also all those video I embed or image I have it setup it to automatically become the thumbnail/card of that note so in the dashboard I can quickly scroll through it and see what its about visually.

### Example Workflow

1.embed lecture notes with timestamp for the topic.( i did this using html)
2.show that embed video file or image as a card that shows up as a thumbnail/card in a dashboard

### Caveats

I don't think I can think of a Caveat, I'm sorry I don't have any experience with this.

### Do you wish to attempt implementing this yourself?

Yes

### Platforms

- [x] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Additional Information

_No response_

summary: No automatic reload of changed files

description.text:
### Description

If I modify a file outside of Zettlr on ubuntu Linux the changes do not load although the corresponding option is ticked in the settings. Activating the watchdog does not help. It works on Windows. On Windows Subsystem for Linux the bug shows up as well.

### Reproducing

Open a file in Zettlr and make sure automated reload of changes is activated. Modify it outside in a different editor. The changes do not show up in Zettlr.

### Zettlr version

3.4.4

### Installation Method

Other (please specify below)

### Installation Method (Other)

https://apt.zettlr.com/

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

ubuntu 24.04

### Additional Information

_No response_

summary: high CPU load (and probably disk access)

description.text:
### Description

If I run Zettlr 3.4.4 on Windows it is constantly using half a core even if no document is open, so I have a load of about 3% on a 24 core machine which seems to be enough to have the fan running all the time. Furthermore the filesystem in use is an encrypted one and I see also the encryption process at 5% which indicates that Zettlr is doing some kind of file access. Whenever I close Zettlr the encryption process goes back to nearly 0. I suspected that the autosave settings are responsible but disabling autosave did not help. The same setup works perfectly on Linux and even on the Windows Subsystem for Linux (mounting the Windows drive using `mount -t drvfs`).

### Reproducing

Open Zettlr on Windows

### Zettlr version

3.4.4

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [x] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Windows 11 23H2

### Additional Information

_No response_

summary: Fixes #5641 : Add select/deselect all files toggle in project export settings

description.text:
<!--
  Thank you for opening up this pull request! Please read this comment carefully
  and make sure to fill in as much info as possible below as well.

  First, the obligatory checklist. You must make sure to follow this list as
  much as possible to make merging your PR as easy as possible:

  1. I documented all behaviour within the code as far as I could.
  2. This PR targets the develop branch and *not* the master branch.
  3. I have tested my changes extensively and paid extra attention to potential
     cross-platform issues (e./g. Cmd/Ctrl/Super key bindings)
  4. I ran the `yarn lint` and `yarn test` commands successfully
  5. I have added an entry to the CHANGELOG.md
  6. I matched my code-style to the repository as far as possible
  7. I agree that my code will be published under the GNUP GPL v3 license
  8. In case I created new files, I added a header to them (see the existing
     files for examples)
  9. Before opening this PR, I ran `git pull` a last time to prevent any merge
     issues

  If you don't know how to fulfill some of the above points, feel free to open
  your PR nevertheless and mention those points where you are unsure. The more
  you can fulfill, the faster we can merge your PR, otherwise it will just take
  longer.
 -->

## Description
Fixes #5641

This PR introduces a "Select/Deselect All Files" toggle button in the Project Settings → Files panel. The feature allows users to quickly select all available files for export or clear the current selection with a single click. This enhancement addresses the usability challenge described in [Issue #5641](https://github.com/Zettlr/Zettlr/issues/5641)

The toggle intelligently switches between:

Select All: Adds all files from the project directory to the export list.

Deselect All: Clears all files from the export list.

The selection state is determined dynamically, so if all files are already selected, clicking the button will deselect them—and vice versa.

This feature improves the workflow for users managing large Zettlr projects by reducing the manual effort required to configure exports.

## Changes

Added a new "Select/Deselect All Files" button to the Files tab in the Project Settings view (source/win-project-properties/App.vue).

Introduced a toggleSelectAllFiles() method to manage bulk selection and deselection of files.

Added a computed property areAllFilesSelected to determine if all project files are currently selected.

UI dynamically updates the button label between “Select All” and “Deselect All” based on the current selection state.

Ensured no breaking changes to existing file selection logic or project export functionality.

## Additional information
This feature addresses issue [#5641](https://github.com/Zettlr/Zettlr/issues/5641) by allowing users to bulk-select or deselect all project files for export with a single click.
It enhances the user experience when managing large projects with numerous files.
The implementation is backward-compatible and introduces no breaking changes.

<!-- Please provide any testing system -->
Tested on Windows 10 with multiple files and verified proper state updates and UI behavior.
